tossups.question: In an opera by this composer, Osmino plots to kill the sultan of Damietta by arming Christian prisoners. During the premiere of another opera by this composer, workers below the stage turned a wheel to shake the stage and simulate an Act 3 shipwreck, which is caused by a slave navigating directly into a storm. This composer created the knight Armando for his last Italian opera, (*) The Crusader in Egypt. A 2018 critical edition of this composer’s final opera revealed the many changes made by François-Joseph Fétis for its Paris premiere, such as moving the central setting to Madagascar. In this composer’s original version of that opera, a man in love with Inès is almost forced to marry an Indian princess, who later commits suidide by inhaling poisonous fumes from the (*) manchineel tree. Other characters from that opera include Nélusko, who sings a ballad about the sea giant Adamastor, and an explorer who sings the aria “O Paradis!” For 10 points, name this composer who depicted the adventures of Vasco da Gama in the grand opera L’Africaine [lah-free-ken].
tossups.answer: Giacomo Meyerbeer [or Jakob Liebmann Beer] (Meyerbeer’s original title was Vasco da Gama)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Giacomo Meyerbeer [or Jakob Liebmann Beer] (Meyerbeer's original title was Vasco da Gama)
tossups.question_sanitized: In an opera by this composer, Osmino plots to kill the sultan of Damietta by arming Christian prisoners. During the premiere of another opera by this composer, workers below the stage turned a wheel to shake the stage and simulate an Act 3 shipwreck, which is caused by a slave navigating directly into a storm. This composer created the knight Armando for his last Italian opera, (*) The Crusader in Egypt. A 2018 critical edition of this composer's final opera revealed the many changes made by Francois-Joseph Fetis for its Paris premiere, such as moving the central setting to Madagascar. In this composer's original version of that opera, a man in love with Ines is almost forced to marry an Indian princess, who later commits suidide by inhaling poisonous fumes from the (*) manchineel tree. Other characters from that opera include Nelusko, who sings a ballad about the sea giant Adamastor, and an explorer who sings the aria "O Paradis!" For 10 points, name this composer who depicted the adventures of Vasco da Gama in the grand opera L'Africaine [lah-free-ken].

tossups.question: An opera by this composer cast the castrato Giambattista as the knight Armando. In one of this composer’s operas, a scandalous ballet in the third act, meant to showcase its venue’s new gas lighting, starred Marie Taglioni as the Abbess Helena. This composer, whose final Italian-language opera was Il crociato in Egitto (“eel kroh-CHA-toh een ay-JEET-toh”), wrote an opera in which the explorer Vasco da Gama sings “O paradis” (“oh pah-rah-DEE”) on Selika’s island. An opera by this composer opens with a short prelude set to Martin Luther’s “Ein feste Burg” (“yne FESS-tuh BOORK”). The libretto for this composer’s opera L’étoile du nord (“lay-TWALL doo NOR”) was written by the inventor of the well-made play, Eugène Scribe (“oo-zhen screeb”). This composer wrote a “Ballet of the Nuns” as well as an opera in which Valentine and her lover Raoul, a member of the title religious group, are killed in the St. Bartholomew’s Day Massacre. For 10 points, name this composer of grand operas such as Robert le Diable (“ro-BAIR luh dee-AH-bluh”) and Les Huguenots (“lay oog-NOH”).

packet.name: C (MSU A, NYU B, UCLA A, and Virginia A)

For 10 points, name this composer of grand operas such as Robert le Diable ("ro-BAIR luh dee-AH-bluh") and Les Huguenots ("lay oog-NOH").

tossups.question: One opera by this composer ends with the cello accompanying a character’s hallucination, in which she is visited by her lover in a swan drawn chariot. Another opera by him ends with Marcel having a vision of heaven accompanied by six harps on stage and a chorus of soldiers singing “God wants blood!” In that opera, a band of the title figures singing the rataplan interrupts the duel between Raoul and Saint-Bris. This composer’s last opera ends with Sélika inhaling the blossoms of the manchineel tree and features a scene in which Nelusko sings the “Ballad of Adamastor.” This man, who often collaborated with librettist Eugène Scribe, wrote an opera about Vasco da Gama, as well as an opera in which the marriage of Margaret of Valois presages the St. Bartholomew’s Day Massacre. For 10 points, name this composer of grand operas, such as L’Africaine and The Huguenots.
tossups.question_sanitized: One opera by this composer ends with the cello accompanying a character's hallucination, in which she is visited by her lover in a swan drawn chariot. In that opera, a band of the title figures singing the rataplan interrupts the duel between Raoul and Saint-Bris. This man, who often collaborated with librettist Eugene Scribe, wrote an opera about Vasco da Gama, as well as an opera in which the marriage of Margaret of Valois presages the St. Bartholomew's Day Massacre. For 10 points, name this composer of grand operas, such as L'Africaine and The Huguenots.

tossups.question: One opera by this man ends with an explosion that destroys a palace in which all the major characters were attending a party. That opera by this man also includes the chorale "Ad nos ad salutarem" and a crazy scene in which a bunch of Anapabtists go figure‐skating. This man, who depicted John of Leyden's takeover of Munster in that opera, was targeted by name in the essay Opera and Drama, two years after another polemic of which he is believed to have been the principal target, (*) On Jewishness in Music. This composer of The Prophet and The Crusader in Egypt also showed the singing of "God wants blood!" and the dramatic tolling of bells in an opera where Raoul and Valentine are among the many killed on St. Bartholomew's Day. For 10 points, name this composer of Les Huguenots.
tossups.question_sanitized: One opera by this man ends with an explosion that destroys a palace in which all the major characters were attending a party. This man, who depicted John of Leyden's takeover of Munster in that opera, was targeted by name in the essay Opera and Drama, two years after another polemic of which he is believed to have been the principal target, (*) On Jewishness in Music. This composer of The Prophet and The Crusader in Egypt also showed the singing of "God wants blood!" and the dramatic tolling of bells in an opera where Raoul and Valentine are among the many killed on St. Bartholomew's Day. For 10 points, name this composer of Les Huguenots.

tossups.question: In one of this man's historical operas, Armando exposes the Grand Vizier's machinations and saves the life of Sultan Aladino during the Sixth Crusade. He also wrote about Konrad switching clothes with Frederick the Great in the finest-ever operatic treatment of the Third Silesian War. This composer of The Crusade in Egypt and A Camp in Silesia also drew on historical incidents in a collaboration with Eugene Scribe that told of a dastardly (*) Norman duke; in an opera about the travels of Vasco da Gama; and in an opera that ends with the St. Bartholomew’s Day Massacre. For 10 points, name this composer of Robert the Devil,L’Africaine, and The Huguenots.
tossups.question_sanitized: In one of this man's historical operas, Armando exposes the Grand Vizier's machinations and saves the life of Sultan Aladino during the Sixth Crusade. He also wrote about Konrad switching clothes with Frederick the Great in the finest-ever operatic treatment of the Third Silesian War. This composer of The Crusade in Egypt and A Camp in Silesia also drew on historical incidents in a collaboration with Eugene Scribe that told of a dastardly (*) Norman duke; in an opera about the travels of Vasco da Gama; and in an opera that ends with the St. Bartholomew's Day Massacre. For 10 points, name this composer of Robert the Devil,L'Africaine, and The Huguenots.

bonuses.leadin: Chopin’s last published piece and only mature chamber work is a G minor sonata for this instrument dedicated to Auguste Franchomme. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Chopin's last published piece and only mature chamber work is a G minor sonata for this instrument dedicated to Auguste Franchomme. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this instrument. The operetta composer Victor Herbert inspired Dvořák to write a concerto for this instrument in B minor.

Franchomme transcribed a piece in this genre by Chopin often nicknamed “Cello” for its heartsick left-hand melody. Chopin’s other pieces in this didactic genre include ones nicknamed “Black Key” and “Revolutionary.”
Franchomme and Chopin jointly wrote a Grand duo concertant for cello and piano based on themes from this Jewish composer’s opera Robert le diable (“roh-BAIR le dee-AHB-luh”).

Franchomme and Chopin jointly wrote a Grand duo concertant for cello and piano based on themes from this Jewish composer's opera Robert le diable ("roh-BAIR le dee-AHB-luh").
answers_sanitized: Giacomo Meyerbeer [or Jakob Liebmann Meyer Beer]

bonuses.leadin: Franz Liszt's fifteenth Hungarian Rhapsody is based on this theme. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this theme, which closes Part I of another work. In that work, the despondent Faust wonders why this theme's subjects are able to seek glory and fame, then almost commits suicide before hearing the Easter Song.

That adaptation of the Rakoczy (rah-koh-CHAY) March appears in The Damnation of Faust, a "dramatic legend" by this French composer. His obsession with Harriet Smithson was portrayed in his Symphonie Fantastique.

Raoul Gunsbourg's staging of the Rakoczy March drew comparison for its similarities to the "Benediction of the Swords" from a grand opera by this composer, in which another Raoul marries Valentine before being killed by St. Bris.

bonuses.leadin: This composer popularized the use of leitmotifs (“LITE-mow-teef”) in his operas. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this notoriously antisemitic German composer who promoted the idea of a Gesamtkunstwerk (“guh-ZAMT-koonst-vairk”), or “total work of art”. This composer’s works include Das Rheingold, part of the massive four-opera long Ring Cycle.

This Wagner opera’s namesake opening chord resolves dissonantly, presaging 20th century atonality. The two title characters of this opera, based on an Arthurian legend, fall in love after drinking a love potion.
Wagner repeatedly attacked this Jewish German composer in his virulent polemic Jewishness in Music. This composer wrote many grand operas such as Robert le diable and Les Huguenots (“hyoo-guh-noh”).
answers: Tristan und Isolde [or Tristan and Isolde; prompt on Tristan chord]
answers_sanitized: Richard Wagner ("REEK-hart VAHG-nur") [or Wilhelm Richard Wagner]
Tristan und Isolde [or Tristan and Isolde; prompt on Tristan chord]

bonuses.leadin: This composer wrote that “among all orchestral instruments, the viola’s excellent qualities have long suffered the most neglect.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author of Treatise on Instrumentation who prominently featured the viola in his symphony Harold in Italy.

Berlioz also praised this composer’s use of gongs and the bassoon’s middle range in the Resurrection of the Nuns scene from his opera Robert le Diable.

bonuses.leadin: The sound holes on the body of this instrument are sometimes referred to as “flaming swords.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this instrument popular during the Baroque period that has six or seven strings played with the bow and another set of sympathetic strings underneath that resonate when the instrument is played.

Although the viola d’amore fell out of use by the 19th century, this composer called for one to accompany the aria “Plus blanc que la blanche hermine” (ploos blahnk kay lah blawnsh air-meen) in his grand opera Les Huguenots. He also composed the opera L’Africaine.

This composer included a partita for the unusual combination of two violas d’amore and continuo in his Harmonia artificioso-ariosa. Like his Mystery Sonatas for the violin, that collection explored the possibilities of scordatura tuning.

bonuses.leadin: Act 3 of this opera contains a “skating” ballet in a forest near Westphalia. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this opera, in which Jean de Leyd is given a title by a trio of scheming Anabaptists, who intend on dethroning Count Oberthal and taking Munster for themselves. At the end of this opera Jean’s lover Berthe sets fire to his palace and subsequently kills herself.
The Prophet is by this German composer of such “grand operas” as Les Huguenots and L’Africaine.

Somewhat ironically, the Jewish Meyerbeer was an early supporter of Richard Wagner, who in turn imitated Meyerbeer so much in this opera that it was called “Meyerbeer’s best opera.” Its title character fights Orsini and Colonna before dying in a fire inside of the Capitol of Rome.

bonuses.leadin: Daniel Auber's The Mute Girl of Portici, which ends with an eruption of Mount Vesuvius, is usually considered to be the first opera in this genre. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this primarily-19th-century genre of dramatic opera, which includes many works with libretti by Eugène Scribe (oo-ZHEN screeb). They are characterized by large casts and extravagant productions.

Another pioneer of the grand opera was this German composer, who included the "Ballet of the Nuns" in his early hit Robert le Diable(roh-BARE luh dee-AH-bluh). He died before the premiere of L'Africaine(la-free-KEN), his opera about Vasco da Gama.
Scribe's libretto for Auber's Gustave III was eventually adapted into A Masked Ball, an opera by this Italian composer whose late-career successes included Otello, Falstaff, and Aida.
answers_sanitized: Giuseppe Verdi [or Giuseppe Fortunino Francesco Verdi]

bonuses.leadin: Act 4 of this opera includes the Grand Duo’s “immortal melody” “Tu l’as dit” and the Blessing of the Daggers, while Act 1 contains the puritanical servant Marcel’s brash battle song “Pif! paf!” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this grand opéra based on a historical event that unfolds despite Queen Marguerite’s attempt at reconciliation, which involves marrying Raoul to Valentine.

At the end of Act 2, Raoul, Nevers, and St.-Bris take this action in a slow section beginning “Par l'honneur” in C minor octaves, as Marcel wishes death on his enemies. A minute after this action, Raoul rejects Valentine, thinking she is Nevers’ immoral mistress, and chaos ensues.
This Prussian Jewish composer fused German orchestration, Italian melody, and French drama in operas like Robert le diable and Les huguenots.
answers: swearing an oath of eternal peace between the Catholics and Huguenots [accept vow, swearing, or serment; prompt (for a specific action) on "making peace" or equivalents]

bonuses.leadin: This operatic excerpt made use of the recently-installed gas lighting at the Paris Opera, and secured the fame of Marie Taglioni. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this episode from the third act of a 1831 opera, in which Bertram goes to a cloister and summons the title vow-breaking ghosts from their tombs, before the Abbess orders them to dance.
The "Ballet of the Nuns" appears in this French composer's grand opera Robert le Diable. He is better known for Le Prophete and Les Huguenots.

The "Ballet of the Nuns" is widely held to be the first work in the romantic ballet genre, which introduced this innovation to the art form.Â The pancake and the bell styles are common variants of this aspect of ballet.
